# CWE-186 negative: regex matches realistic hostnames
lang: go
file: CWE-186-safe.go
---
package sample

import (
	"net/http"
	"regexp"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

var hostPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9]([a-zA-Z0-9-]{0,61}[a-zA-Z0-9])?(\.[a-zA-Z0-9]([a-zA-Z0-9-]{0,61}[a-zA-Z0-9])?)*$`)

// CWE-186 Overly Restrictive Regular Expression (mitigated)
func ValidateUpstream(c *gin.Context) {
	host := c.Query("host")
	if !hostPattern.MatchString(host) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "invalid"})
		return
	}
	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"host": host})
}
